assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response mentions increasing maxSurge to 20 but doesn't explain that GKE has a hard limit of ~20 nodes upgrading simultaneously regardless of the maxSurge setting

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: The response doesn't provide any mathematical calculation showing why 3,000 nodes can't be upgraded in 8 hours based on GKE's parallelism limits

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response recommends extended maintenance windows, split windows across Friday-Saturday, and manual orchestration across weekends

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The upgrade orchestration script shows upgrading CPU pools first, then GPU pools, with specific reasoning about priority order

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: While the response mentions GPU workload considerations and PodDisruptionBudgets, it doesn't specifically recommend timing GPU upgrades during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response explicitly shows extending maintenance windows to 16 hours and using Friday-Saturday split windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention cluster autoscaler limitations or how they interact with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention GKE's node limits or the increased limits for AI workloads in newer versions
